title: Why the Sky is Blue
content:
Have you ever looked up at the sky?

During the day, the sky is blue. But why?

Let me tell you a story about light.

Sunlight looks white. But it is really made of many colors!

Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple light all mix together.

When sunlight comes to Earth, it meets the air.

The air is full of tiny, tiny bits we cannot see.

These tiny bits bounce the light around.

Blue light bounces the most! It scatters everywhere.

That is why when you look up, you see blue all around.

At sunset, the sky turns orange and red.

That is because the light travels farther through the air.

The blue light scatters away. Only red and orange light reach your eyes.

So the sky changes color like magic!

But it is not magic. It is science!

Now you know why the sky is blue.

The End.

author: Educational
